texto: In fact, science is neither materialist nor idealist; it is simply science. It’s impartial and can’t be answerable for the direction of its conclusions.

frase_original: In fact, science is neither materialist nor idealist; it is simply science. It’s impartial and can’t be answerable for the direction of its conclusions.

texto: Depression has its degrees which should be clearly marked. There is an absolute depression, when the very foundation of the psychic mechanism is damaged by pressures and we find it very difficult to recover the joy of life. Quite different is a temporary depression which may, at times, be quite poorly directed, like a wind into the human sails. In order to distinguish between the two we might use the test of human reaction to a material improvement.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Valerian Pidmohylny: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Depression has its degrees which should be clearly marked. There is an absolute depression, when …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, depression, which pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: Depression has its degrees which should be clearly marked. There is an absolute depression, when the very foundation of the psychic mechanism is damaged by pressures and we find it very difficult to recover the joy of life. Quite different is a temporary depression which may, at times, be quite poorly directed, like a wind into the human sails. In order to distinguish between the two we might use the test of human reaction to a material improvement.
